claim_under_audit: HEXA-RIBOZYME has no HARD collision with any pre-existing axis or domain in canon (own 16 single-canonical compliance) and only soft / no overlap with life/crispr-gene-editing AND life/synbio at the architectural-primitive layer.

audit_scope.method: ripgrep-and-jq-style traversal of every _index.json plus full-text grep across domains/**/*.md (case-insensitive); zero-mention quantification across non-biology axes; explicit reading of life/crispr-gene-editing/crispr-gene-editing.md and life/synbio/synbio.md frontmatter + body to confirm scope is sgRNA/Cas RNP / circuit-codon-chassis-metabolic respectively, not catalytic-RNA primitives.

candidate_collision_domains_examined.scope_observed: Cas9 six structural domains / PAM NGG 3+3=6 base recognition / sgRNA / HDR / off-target / delivery / efficacy (Cas RNP gene editing)
candidate_collision_domains_examined.verdict: NO COLLISION at architectural-primitive layer. CRISPR-Cas RNP is a protein-RNA gene-editing complex; ribozyme is a catalytic RNA. Different primary objects (Cas9 protein active site vs RNA active site), different primary constraints (PAM-recognition specificity vs diffusion-limit ceiling), different primary oracle (sgRNA-guided dsDNA cleavage vs RNA-mediated phosphodiester cleavage). At application layer L9 the hexa-ribozyme body explicitly cross-links to life/crispr-gene-editing as a sgRNA-guided-cleavage *route*, which is consistent with sister-axis cross-link not duplication.

candidate_collision_domains_examined.scope_observed: circuit / codon / chassis / metabolic / safety / AI integration
candidate_collision_domains_examined.verdict: NO COLLISION at architectural-primitive layer. HEXA-SYNBIO is circuit/chassis/metabolic engineering; ribozyme is a single-active-site catalytic RNA primitive. At application layer L9 the hexa-ribozyme body cross-links to life/synbio as the in-vitro selection / SELEX *route* for novel ribozyme discovery, which is sister-axis cross-link not duplication.
DOES NOT EXIST in current life axis SSOT. life/_index.json has 54 domains (47 base + 7 fast-track + 1 own#16 sync); none match rna-*/ribosome/translation/spliceosome slugs.

NO COLLISION. No ribozyme/catalytic-RNA term occurrences in cognitive axis. Cross-link from hexa-ribozyme body §11 to cognitive/hexa-mind is speculative L9 RNA-world-cognition-substrate hypothesis, not architectural duplication.
NO COLLISION. Zero ribozyme/RNA-catalysis term occurrences across compute axis 107 domains.

SISTER not collision. Composition genus (multi-strand bundle, P up to 10^4) vs catalysis genus (single active site, 12-30 nt). Object-scale separation 4-7 orders. Primary-constraint orthogonal (Landauer x NP-search ceiling vs diffusion-limit ceiling).

SISTER not collision. Actuation genus (mechanical work output, 10^0-10^2 atoms per actuator) vs catalysis genus (chemical bond cleavage, 10^2-10^3 atoms per active site). Primary-constraint orthogonal (Brownian thermal floor at 310K vs diffusion-limit ceiling).

HARD_COLLISION.definition: same slug OR same architectural-primitive layer mechanism violating own 16 single-canonical

SOFT_COLLISION.definition: same field (chemical-catalysis-by-RNA) but distinct sub-mode (e.g. ribozyme vs RNase P vs ribosome)
SOFT_COLLISION.verdict: NO SOFT COLLISION at the boundary surveyed; all ribozyme sub-modes (hammerhead / hairpin / HDV / group-I / group-II / RNase P / ribosomal PTC) are explicitly co-listed inside hexa-ribozyme.md §1 / §11 corpus and treated as multi-realizability witnesses (raw 106) of one genus, not separate domains.

NO_COLLISION.definition: scope orthogonal at architectural-primitive layer; cross-link is sister-axis or application-route
instances: life/crispr-gene-editing (sister-axis cross-link L9 sgRNA-guided cleavage route)
life/rna-* / life/ribosome / life/translation (DOES NOT EXIST in current SSOT)
compute/* / physics/* / materials/* / space/* / infra/* / culture/* / sf-ufo/* (zero ribozyme keyword occurrences across non-biology axes)

f_rb_5_disposition.rationale: audit method: full _index.json sweep across 11 axes plus full-text keyword grep + direct reading of the two preregistered candidate-collision bodies (life/crispr-gene-editing/crispr-gene-editing.md, life/synbio/synbio.md). Zero ribozyme / hammerhead / RNase P / catalytic-RNA term occurrences outside the 3 biology-axis sister files. life/crispr-gene-editing scope is Cas9 RNP / sgRNA / HDR / off-target / delivery (not catalytic RNA). life/synbio scope is circuit / codon / chassis / metabolic / safety / AI (zero ribozyme references in body). Both are architecturally orthogonal at the primitive layer and properly registered as sister-axis cross-links / application routes in hexa-ribozyme.md §11 DEPENDENCIES. HARD COLLISION 0 -> F-RB-5 RESOLVED per F-RB-5 disposition rule (HARD=0 -> RESOLVED).
f_rb_5_disposition.disposition_rule_followed: task spec step 7: HARD COLLISION 0 -> RESOLVED

remaining_clauses.F-RB-5-sister-axis-collision: RESOLVED — life/crispr-gene-editing does not cover ribozyme-class catalytic-RNA fundamentals; covers Cas9 protein-RNA RNP only.
remaining_clauses.F-RB-5-b: RESOLVED — life/synbio body has zero ribozyme references; hexa-ribozyme cross-links to it as the SELEX route (application layer), not as a primitive-layer duplicate.
remaining_clauses.F-RB-5-c: RESOLVED — no published taxonomy was found in this audit treating clinical gene-editing and architectural-ribozyme catalysis as a single domain; the literature corpus (Cech 1982 / Guerrier-Takada 1983 / Symons 1981 / Wu-Lai 1989 / Steitz 1993 / Nissen-Steitz 2000) treats catalytic RNA as a distinct technical object from CRISPR gene editing.

biology_axis_triangle_revalidation.raw_106_multi_realizability: PASS — HEXA-RIBOZYME genus realised across >=7 channels: hammerhead / hairpin / HDV / group-I intron / group-II intron / RNase P / ribosomal PTC. Threshold raw 106 (>=3 channels) exceeded by >=2.3x.
biology_axis_triangle_revalidation.raw_71_three_falsifiers_per_claim: PASS — 15 falsifiers across 5 measurable claims (3 each).

catalysis.primary_object: single ribozyme active site (~12-30 nt catalytic core)
catalysis.primary_constraint: diffusion-limit ceiling k_cat / K_M ~ 10^8-10^9 M^-1 s^-1

three_way_genus_split_justification.verdict: three primary objects / three primary constraints / three primary oracles — three-way orthogonality holds; each axis pair is non-overlapping at the architectural-primitive layer.

own_16_single_canonical_compliance.rule: own#16 — every domain has exactly one .md SSOT under domains/<axis>/<slug>/<slug>.md and one entry in the corresponding _index.json
own_16_single_canonical_compliance.duplicate_slug_check: no other domain has slug ribozyme / hexa-ribozyme / catalytic-rna across 11 axes

own_doc_lint_13_of_13_revalidation.raw_91_c3_disclosure: 10 own#1 (CJK-present) violations exist in proposals/hexa_weave_*.md files baseline; all unrelated to hexa-ribozyme registration; no new violation introduced by cycle 15 fan-out 3/3 or by this F-RB-5 audit cycle 16.

raw_47_zero_cross_repo: audit was zero-cross-repo; only canon working tree consulted.
raw_91_c3_self_disclosure: audit did NOT execute Bayesian model comparison (deferred to F-RB-2 audit by 2026-09-28); audit did NOT execute external literature crawl beyond the corpus already cited in hexa-ribozyme.md (Cech 1982 / Guerrier-Takada 1983 / Symons 1981 / Wu-Lai 1989 / Buzayan 1986 / Steitz 1993 / Nissen-Steitz 2000); audit relied on full-text grep of canon working tree + scope-reading of life/crispr-gene-editing.md and life/synbio.md (sufficient for HARD/SOFT/NONE classification but does not preclude future taxonomic publications that could re-trigger F-RB-5-c — re-audit by 2027-04-28 recommended). HARD COLLISION = 0 declared honestly under task spec step 6 + raw 91 C3 fabricated NO-COLLISION rejection.
